mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a structural shift in capital allocation from residential to commercial sectors, boosting aggregate corporate profitability and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value exceeding 0.10 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates compress equity multiples and increase borrowing costs, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the next 20 trading days when the federal funds rate increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered tightening episodes, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding business demand and validates corporate revenue trajectories, mechanically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of PAYEMS month-over-month changes fails to predict the sign of 5-day SPX returns with accuracy exceeding 55% over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annualized, aggregate demand outpaces supply constraints, driving profit margin expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not statistically higher than baseline during quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling consumer credit balances and rising industrial production indicates deleveraging firms are reinvesting cash into capacity expansion rather than debt service, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS declines and INDPRO increases fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year walk-forward window, or forward returns in the top decile of signal strength are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment environment where the jobless rate exceeds 8.0 percent, subsequent monthly gains in total employment trigger mean-reversion rallies in equity markets as investors price in Federal Reserve stimulus expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0% and PAYEMS rises above 100k, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 4-year test window.
